1→# bop serve smoke test: curl POST creates card in pending/
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→`bop serve` was recently implemented (`crates/bop-cli/src/serve.rs`). It exposes a
     6→`POST /cards/new` endpoint that creates bop cards. The implementation needs a smoke test
     7→that actually starts the server, POSTs to it, and verifies a card appears in `.cards/pending/`.
     8→
     9→## What to do
    10→
    11→1. Read `crates/bop-cli/src/serve.rs` to understand the current implementation.
    12→2. Add an integration test (or extend existing unit tests) that:
    13→   - Binds the server on a random port (or fixed test port like 18080)
    14→   - POSTs `{"id": "smoke-test-serve", "spec": "# test\nsmoke test spec"}`
    15→   - Asserts HTTP 201 response
    16→   - Asserts `.cards/pending/smoke-test-serve.bop/` directory is created
    17→   - Cleans up after the test
    18→3. Run `cargo test -p bop` to verify the new test passes.
    19→4. Run `make check` — must pass clean.
    20→5. Write `output/result.md` with what was tested and results.
    21→
    22→## Acceptance
    23→
    24→- `cargo test -p bop` passes including the new serve integration test
    25→- `make check` exits 0
    26→- `output/result.md` exists
